\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Builds synchronized quadrature nodes/weights for a pair of touching triangles. 

The class generates {\itshape paired} sampling points (barycentric) on the two triangles together with properly scaled weights so that singular and near-\/singular integrals (e.\+g., double-\/surface integrals with 1/R kernels) are integrable with high accuracy.

After construction, call\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item get\+Points\+Map(0) for triangle \#1 nodes
\item get\+Points\+Map(1) for triangle \#2 nodes
\item \doxylink{classSingCancellation_a1e39f550c512d7f609257be4f9610082}{get\+Weights()} for the weights associated with each {\itshape paired} sample 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{SingCancellation_8h_source_l00040}{40}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{SingCancellation_8h_source}{Sing\+Cancellation.\+h}}.

Sing\+Cancellation\+::\+Sing\+Cancellation (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{int}]{common\+Nodes}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{classTriangle}{Triangle}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{t\+Ptr1}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{classTriangle}{Triangle}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{t\+Ptr2}{, }\item[{int}]{n\+Quadrature}{, }\item[{double(\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*})}]{x\+Quadrature}{\mbox{[}2\mbox{]}, }\item[{double \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{w\+Quadrature}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Construct and immediately generate the paired quadrature for a triangle pair. 

Detects how the triangles touch by {\ttfamily common\+Nodes} and calls the appropriate builder\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item 1 -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} \doxylink{classSingCancellation_ac1258c9e1d2e1981e9ceb22477312cfd}{common\+Vertex()}
\item 2 -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} \doxylink{classSingCancellation_aa72d2a4d4365846ffcbee49b07a4baa7}{common\+Edge()}
\item 3 -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} \doxylink{classSingCancellation_a7d4e2fb258f1a7a5923c823800863983}{common\+Facet()}
\end{DoxyItemize}


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}

\hline
{\em t\+Ptr1} & Pointer to first triangle. \\
\hline
{\em t\+Ptr2} & Pointer to second triangle. \\
\hline
{\em n\+Quadrature} & Number of 1D GL points (typically 1, 4, or 8). \\
\hline
{\em x\+Quadrature} & GL nodes array of shape \mbox{[}n\+Quadrature\mbox{]}\mbox{[}2\mbox{]} with $ (\xi, 1-\xi) $. \\
\hline
{\em w\+Quadrature} & GL weights array of length n\+Quadrature.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::invalid\+\_\+argument} & if {\ttfamily common\+Nodes} not in \{1,2,3\}. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}

\doxysubsection{Member Function Documentation}
\Hypertarget{classSingCancellation_aa72d2a4d4365846ffcbee49b07a4baa7}\index{SingCancellation@{SingCancellation}!commonEdge@{commonEdge}}
\index{commonEdge@{commonEdge}!SingCancellation@{SingCancellation}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{commonEdge()}{commonEdge()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classSingCancellation_aa72d2a4d4365846ffcbee49b07a4baa7} 
void Sing\+Cancellation\+::common\+Edge (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build paired quadrature for triangles sharing a {\itshape common edge}. 

Produces $ 12 n^4 $ paired nodes per the six base mappings plus their symmetric counterparts ( $ \xi \leftrightarrow \eta $). Includes the $ \omega $-\/dependent Jacobian. Based on Taylor (2003), D\textquotesingle{}Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
Called automatically by the constructor when {\ttfamily common\+Nodes==2}.
\end{DoxyNote}
Build quadrature for triangles sharing a {\itshape common edge}. Mapping creates six base cases ( $k=0...5$), then symmetrizes to twelve, yielding $12n^4$ paired nodes overall. The $\omega$-\/dependent Jacobian $ x_1 \omega^2 (1-\omega) $ is folded into the weights.

\begin{DoxyWarning}{Warning}
std\+::runtime\+\_\+error if no common edge is found. 
\end{DoxyWarning}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{commonFacet()}{commonFacet()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classSingCancellation_a7d4e2fb258f1a7a5923c823800863983} 
void Sing\+Cancellation\+::common\+Facet (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build paired quadrature for {\itshape identical triangles} (common facet). 

Produces $ 6 n^4 $ paired nodes from three base mappings and symmetry, with per-\/case Jacobians folded into the weights. Based on Taylor (2003), D\textquotesingle{}Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
Called automatically by the constructor when {\ttfamily common\+Nodes==3}.
\end{DoxyNote}
Build quadrature for {\itshape identical} triangles (common facet). Uses three base mappings and symmetry ( $ \xi \leftrightarrow \eta $) to produce $ 6 n^4 $ pairs. A per-\/case Jacobian (three values) is applied to the weights.

\Hypertarget{classSingCancellation_ac1258c9e1d2e1981e9ceb22477312cfd}\index{SingCancellation@{SingCancellation}!commonVertex@{commonVertex}}
\index{commonVertex@{commonVertex}!SingCancellation@{SingCancellation}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{commonVertex()}{commonVertex()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classSingCancellation_ac1258c9e1d2e1981e9ceb22477312cfd} 
void Sing\+Cancellation\+::common\+Vertex (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build paired quadrature for triangles sharing a {\itshape single vertex}. 

Produces $ 2 n^4 $ paired nodes (symmetrization doubles the base $ n^4 $ set), with weights including the appropriate Jacobian factors arising from the four nested scalar transforms. Based on Taylor (2003), D\textquotesingle{}Elia et al. (2011), Sarraf et al. (2014).

\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
Called automatically by the constructor when {\ttfamily common\+Nodes==1}.
\end{DoxyNote}
Build quadrature for triangles with one {\itshape common vertex}. Creates the raw 4D tensor-\/product rule ( $ n^4 $ points), then applies the two-\/way symmetrization ( $ \xi \leftrightarrow \eta $) to obtain $ 2 n^4 $ paired samples. Each sample produces one barycentric triple on triangle \#1 and the corresponding triple on triangle \#2, plus the combined weight\+: $ w = 4 w_i w_j w_p w_q J(\xi, \eta, ...) $.

Internally uses \doxylink{classTriangle_aa137190a0a6fd9adadee37a8228313d6}{Triangle\+::\+Find\+Common\+Vertex\+Indices()} to determine how the barycentric coordinates must be permuted on each triangle so that the {\itshape same} physical vertex is aligned in both local parameterizations.

\begin{DoxyWarning}{Warning}
On failure to detect the shared vertex, a runtime\+\_\+error is thrown. 
\end{DoxyWarning}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{getPermutation()}{getPermutation()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classSingCancellation_af51fe5685bf1d5a85afd61a50eb375fe} 
std\+::vector$<$ int $>$ Sing\+Cancellation\+::get\+Permutation (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{int}]{shift}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Permutation of barycentric components induced by vertex/edge alignment. 

The shift encodes how the $ (\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \lambda_3) $ triple must be permuted so that the first component corresponds to the vertex opposite the "{}free"{} entity.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em shift} & Encoded shift (−3...−1, 1...3). \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
A permutation vector of indices in \{0,1,2\}.
\end{DoxyReturn}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::invalid\+\_\+argument} & on unsupported {\ttfamily shift}.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}
Map "{}shift code"{} -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} permutation indices of a barycentric triple. Encodes the rotations/reflections needed to align local $ (\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \lambda_3) $ choices across the two triangles so the {\itshape same physical} vertex/edge is addressed.
